name: French A2 Grammar
description: Master CEFR A2 French grammar — scoped from the Référentiel A2 pour le français (Beacco et al., Didier). Fill in the blank to prove you know which form fits.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} de musique.
(I talked about music.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I talked about music.
facts.explanation: Passé composé of parler: avoir (ai) + past participle parlé. Regular -er verb: parl- + -é.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} à des amis.
(I talked to friends.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I talked to friends.
facts.explanation: Passé composé of parler: avoir (ai) + past participle parlé. Same form regardless of what follows the verb.

facts.quizQuestion:
J'ai {___} de musique.
(I talked about music.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I talked about music.
facts.explanation: Past participle of parler (to speak): regular -er verb drops -er and adds -é → parlé.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} du caviar.
(I ate caviar.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I ate caviar.
facts.explanation: Passé composé of manger: avoir (ai) + past participle mangé. Regular -er verb: mang- + -é.

facts.quizQuestion:
J'ai {___} du caviar.
(I ate caviar.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I ate caviar.
facts.explanation: Past participle of manger (to eat): regular -er verb → mangé.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} toute la journée.
(She worked all day.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: She worked all day.
facts.explanation: Passé composé of travailler (il/elle): avoir (a) + participle travaillé. Regular -er verb.

facts.quizQuestion:
Nous avons {___} ensemble hier soir.
(We worked together last night.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: We worked together last night.
facts.explanation: Past participle of travailler (to work): regular -er verb → travaillé.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} un film hier soir.
(They watched a film last night.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: They watched a film last night.
facts.explanation: Passé composé of regarder (ils/elles): avoir (ont) + participle regardé. Regular -er verb.

facts.quizQuestion:
Tu as {___} la télévision ce matin.
(You watched television this morning.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: You watched television this morning.
facts.explanation: Past participle of regarder (to watch): regular -er verb → regardé.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} mon travail à dix heures.
(I finished my work at ten o'clock.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I finished my work at ten o'clock.
facts.explanation: Passé composé of finir: avoir (ai) + past participle fini. Regular -ir verb: fin- + -i.

facts.quizQuestion:
Vous avez {___} l'exercice?
(Have you finished the exercise?)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Have you finished the exercise?
facts.explanation: Past participle of finir (to finish): regular -ir verb drops -ir and adds -i → fini.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} ce restaurant pour le dîner.
(We chose this restaurant for dinner.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: We chose this restaurant for dinner.
facts.explanation: Passé composé of choisir (nous): avoir (avons) + participle choisi. Regular -ir verb.

facts.quizQuestion:
Elle a {___} une robe bleue.
(She chose a blue dress.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: She chose a blue dress.
facts.explanation: Past participle of choisir (to choose): regular -ir verb → chois- + -i → choisi.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} mes devoirs ce matin.
(I did my homework this morning.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I did my homework this morning.
facts.explanation: Passé composé of faire (to do/make): avoir (ai) + irregular participle fait.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il a {___} une erreur.
(He made a mistake.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: He made a mistake.
facts.explanation: Irregular past participle of faire (to do/make): not faisé — the participle is fait.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} quelque chose d'important.
(You said something important.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: You said something important.
facts.explanation: Passé composé of dire (to say) with tu: avoir (as) + irregular participle dit.

facts.quizQuestion:
Qu'est-ce qu'elle a {___}?
(What did she say?)
facts.sentenceTranslation: What did she say?
facts.explanation: Irregular past participle of dire (to say): not diré — the participle is dit.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} ma décision.
(I made my decision.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I made my decision.
facts.explanation: Passé composé of prendre (to take): avoir (ai) + irregular participle pris.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} froid hier.
(I caught a cold yesterday.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I caught a cold yesterday.
facts.explanation: Prendre froid (to catch a cold) uses prendre in passé composé: avoir (ai) + participle pris.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il a {___} le bus pour aller au travail.
(He took the bus to go to work.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: He took the bus to go to work.
facts.explanation: Irregular past participle of prendre (to take): not prendu — the participle is pris.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} combien de temps pour arriver?
(How long did you take to arrive?)
facts.sentenceTranslation: How long did you take to arrive?
facts.explanation: Passé composé of mettre (to put/take time) with vous: avoir (avez) + irregular participle mis.

facts.quizQuestion:
Elle a {___} sa veste sur la chaise.
(She put her jacket on the chair.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: She put her jacket on the chair.
facts.explanation: Irregular past participle of mettre (to put): not metté — the participle is mis.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} une lettre à sa mère.
(He wrote a letter to his mother.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: He wrote a letter to his mother.
facts.explanation: Passé composé of écrire (to write) with il: avoir (a) + irregular participle écrit.

facts.quizQuestion:
Nous avons {___} un email au professeur.
(We wrote an email to the teacher.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: We wrote an email to the teacher.
facts.explanation: Irregular past participle of écrire (to write): not écrié — the participle is écrit.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} ce livre?
(Did you read this book?)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Did you read this book?
facts.explanation: Passé composé of lire (to read) with tu: avoir (as) + irregular participle lu.

facts.quizQuestion:
Elle a {___} un article intéressant.
(She read an interesting article.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: She read an interesting article.
facts.explanation: Irregular past participle of lire (to read): not liré — the participle is lu.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} un chien.
(I saw a dog.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I saw a dog.
facts.explanation: Passé composé of voir (to see): avoir (ai) + irregular participle vu.

facts.quizQuestion:
Ils ont {___} un film hier soir.
(They saw a film last night.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: They saw a film last night.
facts.explanation: Irregular past participle of voir (to see): not voyé — the participle is vu.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} beaucoup de succès dans sa vie.
(He experienced a lot of success in his life.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: He experienced a lot of success in his life.
facts.explanation: Passé composé of connaître (to know/experience) with il: avoir (a) + irregular participle connu.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} de la chance ce jour-là.
(We were lucky that day.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: We were lucky that day.
facts.explanation: Passé composé of avoir (to have) with nous: avoir (avons) + irregular participle eu. Avoir uses itself as auxiliary.

facts.quizQuestion:
J'ai {___} un problème avec ma voiture.
(I had a problem with my car.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I had a problem with my car.
facts.explanation: Irregular past participle of avoir (to have): not avé — the participle is eu (highly irregular form).

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} malade pendant une semaine.
(He was sick for a week.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: He was sick for a week.
facts.explanation: Passé composé of être (to be) uses avoir as auxiliary: avoir (a) + irregular participle été.

facts.quizQuestion:
Tu as {___} à Paris l'année dernière?
(Were you in Paris last year?)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Were you in Paris last year?
facts.explanation: Irregular past participle of être (to be): été. This is among the most important irregular participles.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} lui téléphoner mais il n'était pas là.
(I wanted to call him but he wasn't there.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I wanted to call him but he wasn't there.
facts.explanation: Passé composé of vouloir (to want): avoir (ai) + irregular participle voulu.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} finir le projet à temps.
(She was able to finish the project on time.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: She was able to finish the project on time.
facts.explanation: Passé composé of pouvoir (to be able to) with elle: avoir (a) + irregular participle pu.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} répondre à toutes les questions.
(You managed to answer all the questions.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: You managed to answer all the questions.
facts.explanation: Passé composé of savoir (to know/manage) with tu: avoir (as) + irregular participle su.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} l'équitation.
(I experienced horse riding.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I experienced horse riding.
facts.explanation: Passé composé of essayer (to try): avoir (ai) + past participle essayé. Regular -er verb: essay- + -é.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} l'examen.
(I failed the exam.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I failed the exam.
facts.explanation: Passé composé of rater (to fail/miss): avoir (ai) + past participle raté. Regular -er verb.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___}.
(I've had a good sleep.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I've had a good sleep.
facts.explanation: Passé composé of dormir (to sleep): avoir (ai) + past participle dormi. Irregular -ir verb: dorm- + -i.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___}.
(I bought it.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I bought it.
facts.explanation: Passé composé of acheter (to buy): avoir (ai) + participle acheté. COD pronoun l' precedes the auxiliary.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} du thé après le repas.
(They drank tea after the meal.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: They drank tea after the meal.
facts.explanation: Passé composé of boire (to drink) with elles: avoir (ont) + irregular participle bu.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il a {___} tout le lait.
(He drank all the milk.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: He drank all the milk.
facts.explanation: Irregular past participle of boire (to drink): not buvé — the participle is bu.

facts.quizQuestion:
Ken {___} à Kyoto.
(Ken has arrived in Kyoto.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Ken has arrived in Kyoto.
facts.explanation: est arrivé — arriver takes être as auxiliary. Masc. sing.: être (est) + arrivé, no agreement suffix.

facts.quizQuestion:
Lincoln {___} en 1865.
(Lincoln died in 1865.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Lincoln died in 1865.
facts.explanation: est mort — mourir takes être. Irregular past participle: mort. Masc. sing.: no agreement suffix.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il {___} dans l'Ohio.
(He was born in Ohio.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: He was born in Ohio.
facts.explanation: est né — naître takes être. Irregular past participle: né. Masc. sing.: no agreement suffix.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il {___} à Hokkaïdo.
(He has gone to Hokkaido.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: He has gone to Hokkaido.
facts.explanation: est allé — aller takes être. Past participle: allé. Masc. sing.: no agreement suffix.

facts.quizQuestion:
Elle {___} de là.
(She came out of there.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: She came out of there.
facts.grammarPointLabel: venir (elle) — passé composé avec être + accord fém. sing.
facts.explanation: est venue — venir takes être. Past participle: venu. Fem. sing. agreement: venu + e = venue.

facts.quizQuestion:
Paris {___} en 1940.
(Paris fell in 1940.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Paris fell in 1940.
facts.grammarPointLabel: tomber (elle/Paris) — passé composé avec être + accord fém. sing.
facts.explanation: est tombée — tomber takes être. Paris is grammatically feminine. Fem. sing. agreement: tombé + e = tombée.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il {___} par terre.
(He fell down on the floor.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: He fell down on the floor.
facts.explanation: est tombé — tomber takes être. Past participle: tombé. Masc. sing.: no agreement suffix.

facts.quizQuestion:
Marie {___} tôt ce matin.
(Marie left early this morning.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Marie left early this morning.
facts.grammarPointLabel: partir (elle) — passé composé avec être + accord fém. sing.
facts.explanation: est partie — partir takes être. Past participle: parti. Fem. sing. agreement: parti + e = partie.

facts.quizQuestion:
Mon père {___} en voyage hier.
(My father left on a trip yesterday.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: My father left on a trip yesterday.
facts.explanation: est parti — partir takes être. Past participle: parti. Masc. sing.: no agreement suffix.

facts.quizQuestion:
La directrice {___} dans la salle.
(The director entered the room.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: The director entered the room.
facts.grammarPointLabel: entrer (elle) — passé composé avec être + accord fém. sing.
facts.explanation: est entrée — entrer takes être. Past participle: entré. Fem. sing. agreement: entré + e = entrée.

facts.quizQuestion:
Le médecin {___} sans frapper.
(The doctor entered without knocking.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: The doctor entered without knocking.
facts.explanation: est entré — entrer takes être. Past participle: entré. Masc. sing.: no agreement suffix.

facts.quizQuestion:
Sophie {___} avec ses amies hier soir.
(Sophie went out with her friends last night.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Sophie went out with her friends last night.
facts.grammarPointLabel: sortir (elle) — passé composé avec être + accord fém. sing.
facts.explanation: est sortie — sortir takes être. Past participle: sorti. Fem. sing. agreement: sorti + e = sortie.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il {___} du bureau à dix-huit heures.
(He left the office at six o'clock.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: He left the office at six o'clock.
facts.explanation: est sorti — sortir takes être. Past participle: sorti. Masc. sing.: no agreement suffix.

facts.quizQuestion:
Elle {___} au troisième étage.
(She went up to the third floor.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: She went up to the third floor.
facts.grammarPointLabel: monter (elle) — passé composé avec être + accord fém. sing.
facts.explanation: est montée — monter takes être when used intransitively. Past participle: monté. Fem. sing. agreement: monté + e = montée.

facts.quizQuestion:
Le chat {___} sur le toit.
(The cat climbed onto the roof.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: The cat climbed onto the roof.
facts.explanation: est monté — monter takes être when used intransitively. Past participle: monté. Masc. sing.: no agreement suffix.

facts.quizQuestion:
Grand-mère {___} de l'escalier lentement.
(Grandma came down the stairs slowly.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Grandma came down the stairs slowly.
facts.grammarPointLabel: descendre (elle) — passé composé avec être + accord fém. sing.
facts.explanation: est descendue — descendre takes être when intransitive. Past participle: descendu. Fem. sing. agreement: descendu + e = descendue.

facts.quizQuestion:
Le skieur {___} la piste rapidement.
(The skier came down the slope quickly.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: The skier came down the slope quickly.
facts.explanation: est descendu — descendre takes être when intransitive. Past participle: descendu. Masc. sing.: no agreement suffix.

facts.quizQuestion:
Emma {___} à la maison toute la journée.
(Emma stayed home all day.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Emma stayed home all day.
facts.grammarPointLabel: rester (elle) — passé composé avec être + accord fém. sing.
facts.explanation: est restée — rester takes être. Past participle: resté. Fem. sing. agreement: resté + e = restée.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il {___} calme pendant l'examen.
(He remained calm during the exam.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: He remained calm during the exam.
facts.explanation: est resté — rester takes être. Past participle: resté. Masc. sing.: no agreement suffix.

facts.quizQuestion:
Elle {___} médecin après ses études.
(She became a doctor after her studies.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: She became a doctor after her studies.
facts.grammarPointLabel: devenir (elle) — passé composé avec être + accord fém. sing.
facts.explanation: est devenue — devenir takes être. Irregular past participle: devenu. Fem. sing. agreement: devenu + e = devenue.

facts.quizQuestion:
Le village {___} une grande ville.
(The village became a large city.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: The village became a large city.
facts.explanation: est devenu — devenir takes être. Irregular past participle: devenu. Masc. sing.: no agreement suffix.

facts.quizQuestion:
Ma sœur {___} au cinéma samedi soir.
(My sister went to the cinema Saturday night.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: My sister went to the cinema Saturday night.
facts.grammarPointLabel: aller (elle) — passé composé avec être + accord fém. sing.
facts.explanation: est allée — aller takes être. Past participle: allé. Fem. sing. agreement: allé + e = allée.

facts.quizQuestion:
Nous {___} à la plage hier. (groupe mixte)
(We went to the beach yesterday.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: We went to the beach yesterday.
facts.grammarPointLabel: aller (nous) — passé composé avec être + accord masc./fém. plur.
facts.explanation: sommes allés — nous form of être is sommes. Mixed or all-male group: allé + s = allés. All-female group: allées.

facts.quizQuestion:
Mes parents {___} me rendre visite.
(My parents came to visit me.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: My parents came to visit me.
facts.grammarPointLabel: venir (ils) — passé composé avec être + accord masc. plur.
facts.explanation: sont venus — ils/elles form of être is sont. Masc. plural agreement: venu + s = venus.

facts.quizQuestion:
Les filles {___} en vacances ensemble.
(The girls left on holiday together.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: The girls left on holiday together.
facts.grammarPointLabel: partir (elles) — passé composé avec être + accord fém. plur.
facts.explanation: sont parties — elles form of être is sont. Fem. plural agreement: parti + es = parties.

facts.quizQuestion:
Les étudiantes {___} en retard ce matin.
(The students arrived late this morning.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: The students arrived late this morning.
facts.grammarPointLabel: arriver (elles) — passé composé avec être + accord fém. plur.
facts.explanation: sont arrivées — les étudiantes is feminine plural. Fem. plural agreement: arrivé + es = arrivées.

facts.quizQuestion:
Ma mère {___} en Bretagne.
(My mother was born in Brittany.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: My mother was born in Brittany.
facts.grammarPointLabel: naître (elle) — passé composé avec être + accord fém. sing.
facts.explanation: est née — naître takes être. Irregular past participle: né. Fem. sing. agreement: né + e = née.

facts.quizQuestion:
La reine {___} en 1603.
(The queen died in 1603.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: The queen died in 1603.
facts.grammarPointLabel: mourir (elle) — passé composé avec être + accord fém. sing.
facts.explanation: est morte — mourir takes être. Irregular past participle: mort. Fem. sing. agreement: mort + e = morte.

facts.quizQuestion:
Les feuilles {___} des arbres en automne.
(The leaves fell from the trees in autumn.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: The leaves fell from the trees in autumn.
facts.grammarPointLabel: tomber (elles/feuilles) — passé composé avec être + accord fém. plur.
facts.explanation: sont tombées — les feuilles is feminine plural. Fem. plural agreement: tombé + es = tombées.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je {___} au supermarché ce matin. (locuteur masc.)
(I went to the supermarket this morning.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I went to the supermarket this morning.
facts.grammarPointLabel: aller (je) — passé composé avec être + accord masc. sing.
facts.explanation: suis allé — je form of être is suis. Masc. speaker: allé (no extra suffix). Fem. speaker: suis allée.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je {___} te voir hier soir. (locutrice fém.)
(I came to see you last night.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I came to see you last night.
facts.grammarPointLabel: venir (je) — passé composé avec être + accord fém. sing.
facts.explanation: suis venue — je form of être is suis. Fem. speaker: venu + e = venue. Masc. speaker: suis venu.

facts.quizQuestion:
Ils {___} en France pendant les vacances.
(They stayed in France during the holidays.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: They stayed in France during the holidays.
facts.grammarPointLabel: rester (ils) — passé composé avec être + accord masc. plur.
facts.explanation: sont restés — ils form of être is sont. Masc. plural agreement: resté + s = restés.

facts.quizQuestion:
Quand j'étais enfant, je {___} tout le temps.
(When I was a child, I talked all the time.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: When I was a child, I talked all the time.
facts.explanation: parlais — je form of parler in the imparfait. Regular -er verb: stem parl- + -ais. Valeur: habitude passée (quand j'étais enfant signals repeated past action).

facts.quizQuestion:
Tu {___} trop vite, je ne comprenais pas.
(You were talking too fast, I didn't understand.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: You were talking too fast, I didn't understand.
facts.explanation: parlais — tu form of parler in the imparfait. Stem parl- + -ais (same ending as je). Valeur: action en cours décrivant le contexte d'un autre imparfait.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il {___} souvent de son pays natal.
(He often talked about his home country.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: He often talked about his home country.
facts.explanation: parlait — il/elle/on form of parler in the imparfait. Stem parl- + -ait. The adverb souvent confirms habitual past action. Valeur: habitude passée.

facts.quizQuestion:
Nous {___} de nos projets quand il est entré.
(We were talking about our plans when he came in.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: We were talking about our plans when he came in.
facts.explanation: parlions — nous form of parler in the imparfait. Stem parl- + -ions. Valeur: action en cours (imparfait) interrompue par un événement soudain au passé composé (est entré).

facts.quizQuestion:
Vous {___} français à la maison ?
(Were you speaking French at home?)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Were you speaking French at home?
facts.explanation: parliez — vous form of parler in the imparfait. Stem parl- + -iez. Valeur: description ou question sur une habitude ou situation passée.

facts.quizQuestion:
Ils {___} encore quand nous sommes partis.
(They were still talking when we left.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: They were still talking when we left.
facts.explanation: parlaient — ils/elles form of parler in the imparfait. Stem parl- + -aient. Valeur: action en cours au moment d'une autre action (passé composé: sommes partis).

facts.quizQuestion:
Quand j'étais petit, je {___} toujours des légumes.
(When I was little, I always ate vegetables.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: When I was little, I always ate vegetables.
facts.explanation: mangeais — je form of manger in the imparfait. Spelling note: the -e- is kept after g before -a- to preserve the soft /ʒ/ sound: mange- + -ais. Valeur: habitude passée dans l'enfance.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il {___} une pomme quand je suis arrivé.
(He was eating an apple when I arrived.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: He was eating an apple when I arrived.
facts.explanation: mangeait — il/elle form of manger in the imparfait. Spelling-preservation e stays: mange- + -ait. Valeur: action en cours interrompue par une action au passé composé (suis arrivé).

facts.quizQuestion:
L'an dernier, je {___} dans un café.
(Last year, I was working in a café.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Last year, I was working in a café.
facts.explanation: travaillais — je form of travailler in the imparfait. Regular -er: stem travaill- + -ais. Valeur: description d'une situation professionnelle passée.

facts.quizQuestion:
Mon père {___} beaucoup quand nous étions jeunes.
(My father worked a lot when we were young.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: My father worked a lot when we were young.
facts.explanation: travaillait — il/elle form of travailler in the imparfait. Stem travaill- + -ait. Valeur: description d'une habitude ou situation passée durable.

facts.quizQuestion:
Avant, j'{___} à Lyon avec mes parents.
(Before, I used to live in Lyon with my parents.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Before, I used to live in Lyon with my parents.
facts.explanation: habitais — je form of habiter in the imparfait. Regular -er: stem habit- + -ais. Avant (before/previously) is a classic imparfait time signal for describing past situations. Valeur: description d'une situation de vie passée.

facts.quizQuestion:
Ils {___} près de la mer quand ils étaient enfants.
(They lived near the sea when they were children.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: They lived near the sea when they were children.
facts.explanation: habitaient — ils/elles form of habiter in the imparfait. Stem habit- + -aient. Valeur: description d'une situation passée (lieu de vie dans l'enfance).

facts.quizQuestion:
Il {___} l'image.
(He looked at the picture.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: He looked at the picture.
facts.explanation: regardait — il/elle form of regarder in the imparfait. Regular -er: stem regard- + -ait. Valeur: description d'une action en cours dans le passé.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je {___} la télé quand le téléphone a sonné.
(I was watching TV when the phone rang.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I was watching TV when the phone rang.
facts.explanation: regardais — je form of regarder in the imparfait. Stem regard- + -ais. Valeur: action en cours (imparfait) interrompue par un événement soudain (passé composé: a sonné).

facts.quizQuestion:
Nous {___} souvent des films le vendredi soir.
(We often watched movies on Friday evenings.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: We often watched movies on Friday evenings.
facts.explanation: regardions — nous form of regarder in the imparfait. Stem regard- + -ions. Souvent confirms this is a recurrent past habit. Valeur: habitude passée régulière.

facts.quizQuestion:
J'{___} occupé hier.
(I was busy yesterday.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I was busy yesterday.
facts.explanation: étais — je form of être in the imparfait. Être has an irregular stem ét- (built from the nous present: nous sommes → ét- from Latin erat). Endings are regular: ét- + -ais. Valeur: description d'un état passé.

facts.quizQuestion:
Hier, j'{___} malade.
(I was ill yesterday.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I was ill yesterday.
facts.explanation: étais — je form of être in the imparfait. Irregular stem ét- + -ais. Valeur: description d'un état physique ou de santé passé.

facts.quizQuestion:
J'{___} dans les montagnes.
(I was in the mountains.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I was in the mountains.
facts.explanation: étais — je form of être in the imparfait. Stem ét- + -ais. Valeur: description d'une localisation dans le passé.

facts.quizQuestion:
J'{___} un peu surpris.
(I was a little surprised.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I was a little surprised.
facts.explanation: étais — je form of être in the imparfait. Stem ét- + -ais. Valeur: description d'un état émotionnel passé (surprise, joie, peur).

facts.quizQuestion:
Tu {___} étudiant à Paris, non ?
(You were a student in Paris, right?)
facts.sentenceTranslation: You were a student in Paris, right?
facts.explanation: étais — tu form of être in the imparfait. Same form as je: stem ét- + -ais. Valeur: question sur une situation ou profession passée.

facts.quizQuestion:
C'{___} un cauchemar.
(It was a nightmare.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: It was a nightmare.
facts.explanation: était — c'était is the imparfait form of c'est. Stem ét- + -ait. C'était is essential for describing past situations and giving past context. Valeur: description d'une situation ou expérience passée.

facts.quizQuestion:
C'{___} un méchant lapin.
(That was an evil bunny.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: That was an evil bunny.
facts.explanation: était — c'était is the standard imparfait form for C'est (it is / that is). Stem ét- + -ait. Valeur: description d'un personnage ou d'un objet dans le passé.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il {___} professeur dans une grande école.
(He was a teacher at a prestigious school.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: He was a teacher at a prestigious school.
facts.explanation: était — il/elle form of être in the imparfait. Stem ét- + -ait. Professions in the past use être in the imparfait without an article (Il était professeur, not Il était un professeur). Valeur: description d'un état ou d'une profession passée.

facts.quizQuestion:
Nous {___} très fatigués après la randonnée.
(We were very tired after the hike.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: We were very tired after the hike.
facts.explanation: étions — nous form of être in the imparfait. Stem ét- + -ions. Note the -i- distinguishes nous from singular forms (étais/était). Valeur: description d'un état passé.

facts.quizQuestion:
Où {___}-vous quand ça s'est passé ?
(Where were you when it happened?)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Where were you when it happened?
facts.explanation: étiez — vous form of être in the imparfait. Stem ét- + -iez. Valeur: question sur une localisation passée en contraste avec un événement au passé composé (s'est passé).

facts.quizQuestion:
Ils {___} jeunes quand la guerre a commencé.
(They were young when the war started.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: They were young when the war started.
facts.explanation: étaient — ils/elles form of être in the imparfait. Stem ét- + -aient. Valeur: description d'un état en contraste avec un événement au passé composé (a commencé).

facts.quizQuestion:
Quand j'étais enfant, j'{___} un chien.
(When I was a child, I had a dog.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: When I was a child, I had a dog.
facts.explanation: avais — je form of avoir in the imparfait. Avoir uses the nous-present stem av- (nous avons): av- + -ais. Valeur: description d'une possession passée, très fréquent avec quand j'étais enfant.

facts.quizQuestion:
Tu {___} l'air fatigué ce matin-là.
(You looked tired that morning.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: You looked tired that morning.
facts.explanation: avais — tu form of avoir in the imparfait. Stem av- + -ais (same form as je). Avoir l'air + adjective = to look / to seem. Valeur: description d'un aspect physique passé.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il {___} peur du noir quand il était petit.
(He was afraid of the dark when he was little.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: He was afraid of the dark when he was little.
facts.explanation: avait — il/elle form of avoir in the imparfait. Stem av- + -ait. Avoir peur de + noun = to be afraid of. Valeur: description d'une peur ou d'un état permanent dans l'enfance.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il {___} une belle voix quand il chantait.
(He had a beautiful voice when he sang.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: He had a beautiful voice when he sang.
facts.explanation: avait — il/elle form of avoir in the imparfait. Stem av- + -ait. Both verbs use imparfait (avait, chantait) for simultaneous ongoing past states or habits. Valeur: description d'une qualité passée.

facts.quizQuestion:
Nous n'{___} pas assez d'argent pour partir en vacances.
(We didn't have enough money to go on holiday.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: We didn't have enough money to go on holiday.
facts.explanation: avions — nous form of avoir in the imparfait. Stem av- + -ions. Valeur: description d'une situation matérielle passée (manque de ressources).

facts.quizQuestion:
Vous {___} l'habitude de vous lever tôt ?
(Did you used to be in the habit of getting up early?)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Did you used to be in the habit of getting up early?
facts.explanation: aviez — vous form of avoir in the imparfait. Stem av- + -iez. Avoir l'habitude de + infinitive = to be in the habit of doing something. Valeur: habitude passée.

facts.quizQuestion:
Ils {___} trois enfants et une grande maison.
(They had three children and a big house.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: They had three children and a big house.
facts.explanation: avaient — ils/elles form of avoir in the imparfait. Stem av- + -aient. Valeur: description d'une situation familiale ou matérielle passée.

facts.quizQuestion:
Heureusement il {___} beau.
(Fortunately, the weather was good.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Fortunately, the weather was good.
facts.explanation: faisait — il form of faire in the imparfait. Faire uses the irregular stem fais- (from nous faisons): fais- + -ait. Il faisait + weather adjective (beau, chaud, froid) is the standard way to describe past weather. Valeur: description du passé (météo).

facts.quizQuestion:
Il {___} chaud hier.
(It was hot yesterday.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: It was hot yesterday.
facts.explanation: faisait — il form of faire in the imparfait. Stem fais- + -ait. Il faisait chaud (it was hot) is paired with il faisait froid (it was cold) as the two most common past weather expressions. Valeur: description météorologique.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il {___} froid hier.
(It was cold yesterday.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: It was cold yesterday.
facts.explanation: faisait — il form of faire in the imparfait. Stem fais- + -ait. Il faisait froid is the standard expression for cold weather in a past description. Valeur: description météorologique.

facts.quizQuestion:
Quand j'étais petit, je {___} au foot tous les jours.
(When I was little, I used to play football every day.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: When I was little, I used to play football every day.
facts.explanation: jouais — je form of jouer in the imparfait. Regular -er: stem jou- + -ais. Quand j'étais petit + imparfait is a classic structure for expressing childhood habits in French. Valeur: habitude passée.

facts.quizQuestion:
Chaque été, nous {___} à la mer.
(Every summer, we used to go to the seaside.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Every summer, we used to go to the seaside.
facts.explanation: allions — nous form of aller in the imparfait. Aller uses stem all- (from nous allons): all- + -ions. Chaque + time word is a strong habitual past signal. Valeur: habitude passée régulière.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je {___} quand le réveil a sonné.
(I was sleeping when the alarm went off.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I was sleeping when the alarm went off.
facts.explanation: dormais — je form of dormir in the imparfait. Dormir uses stem dorm- (nous dormons): dorm- + -ais. The imparfait describes the ongoing background action; the passé composé (a sonné) interrupts it. Valeur: action en cours interrompue.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je {___} quand il a frappé à la porte.
(I was reading when he knocked at the door.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I was reading when he knocked at the door.
facts.explanation: lisais — je form of lire in the imparfait. Lire uses stem lis- (nous lisons): lis- + -ais. The imparfait sets the background; a frappé is the interrupting event at a passé composé. Valeur: action en cours interrompue.

facts.quizQuestion:
Hier, j'{___} heureux.
(I was happy yesterday.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I was happy yesterday.
facts.explanation: étais — je form of être in the imparfait. Stem ét- + -ais. Emotional states (heureux, triste, content, surpris) in the past always use être in the imparfait. Valeur: description d'un état émotionnel passé.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il {___} demain.
(It'll snow tomorrow.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: It'll snow tomorrow.
facts.explanation: neigera — il/elle form of neiger. Regular -er verb: keep infinitive as stem, neiger + -a.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je {___} quand John viendra.
(I will leave when John comes.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I will leave when John comes.
facts.explanation: partirai — je form of partir. Regular -ir verb: keep infinitive as stem, partir + -ai.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je partirai quand John {___}.
(I will leave when John comes.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I will leave when John comes.
facts.explanation: viendra — il/elle form of venir. Irregular stem: viendr- + -a. Stem changes entirely from the infinitive.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je {___} bientôt de retour.
(I will be back soon.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I will be back soon.
facts.explanation: serai — je form of être at futur simple. Completely irregular stem: ser- + -ai. Must be memorised.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je {___} libre ce soir.
(I'll be free this evening.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I'll be free this evening.
facts.explanation: serai — je form of être. Irregular stem: ser- + -ai. Same stem across all persons: serai/seras/sera/serons/serez/seront.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il {___} beau cet après-midi.
(It will be fine this afternoon.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: It will be fine this afternoon.
facts.explanation: fera — il/elle form of faire. Irregular stem: fer- + -a. The -ai- of faire is shortened to fer-.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je ne le {___} jamais.
(I will never see him.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I will never see him.
facts.explanation: verrai — je form of voir. Irregular stem: verr- + -ai. Note the double-r in the stem.

facts.quizQuestion:
Demain je {___} à mon professeur.
(Tomorrow I will speak to my teacher.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Tomorrow I will speak to my teacher.
facts.explanation: parlerai — je form of parler. Regular -er verb: keep infinitive parler + -ai. The -er ending stays in the stem.

facts.quizQuestion:
Tu {___} français couramment un jour.
(You will speak French fluently one day.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: You will speak French fluently one day.
facts.explanation: parleras — tu form of parler. Regular -er: parler + -as.

facts.quizQuestion:
Nous {___} de ce problème demain.
(We will talk about this problem tomorrow.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: We will talk about this problem tomorrow.
facts.explanation: parlerons — nous form of parler. Regular -er: parler + -ons.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il {___} son travail ce soir.
(He will finish his work this evening.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: He will finish his work this evening.
facts.explanation: finira — il/elle form of finir. Regular -ir verb: keep infinitive finir + -a.

facts.quizQuestion:
Vous {___} le projet avant vendredi.
(You will finish the project before Friday.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: You will finish the project before Friday.
facts.explanation: finirez — vous form of finir. Regular -ir: finir + -ez.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je {___} le train de huit heures.
(I will take the eight o'clock train.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I will take the eight o'clock train.
facts.explanation: prendrai — je form of prendre. For -re verbs, drop the final -e before adding endings: prendr + -ai.

facts.quizQuestion:
Ils {___} leur décision demain matin.
(They will make their decision tomorrow morning.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: They will make their decision tomorrow morning.
facts.explanation: prendront — ils/elles form of prendre. Drop final -e of -re infinitive: prendr + -ont.

facts.quizQuestion:
Nous {___} à Paris la semaine prochaine.
(We will be in Paris next week.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: We will be in Paris next week.
facts.explanation: serons — nous form of être. Irregular stem: ser- + -ons. Same irregular stem across all persons.

facts.quizQuestion:
J'{___} vingt ans en juillet.
(I will be twenty in July.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I will be twenty in July.
facts.explanation: aurai — je form of avoir. Irregular stem: aur- + -ai. The -v- of avoir disappears entirely.

facts.quizQuestion:
Tu {___} les résultats demain.
(You will have the results tomorrow.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: You will have the results tomorrow.
facts.explanation: auras — tu form of avoir. Irregular stem: aur- + -as.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je {___} la cuisine ce soir.
(I will cook this evening.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I will cook this evening.
facts.explanation: ferai — je form of faire. Irregular stem: fer- + -ai. The -ai- of faire is shortened to fer-.

facts.quizQuestion:
Nous {___} une promenade demain matin.
(We will go for a walk tomorrow morning.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: We will go for a walk tomorrow morning.
facts.explanation: ferons — nous form of faire. Irregular stem: fer- + -ons.

facts.quizQuestion:
L'été prochain, j'{___} en France.
(Next summer, I will go to France.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Next summer, I will go to France.
facts.explanation: irai — je form of aller. Completely irregular stem: ir- + -ai. The infinitive aller changes entirely.

facts.quizQuestion:
Mes parents {___} à la mer en août.
(My parents will go to the seaside in August.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: My parents will go to the seaside in August.
facts.explanation: iront — ils/elles form of aller. Irregular stem: ir- + -ont.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je {___} te chercher à dix-huit heures.
(I will come and pick you up at six o'clock.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I will come and pick you up at six o'clock.
facts.explanation: viendrai — je form of venir. Irregular stem: viendr- + -ai. The stem bears the original -iendr- pattern.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je {___} t'aider demain si tu veux.
(I will be able to help you tomorrow if you want.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I will be able to help you tomorrow if you want.
facts.explanation: pourrai — je form of pouvoir. Irregular stem: pourr- + -ai. Double-r is characteristic of this stem.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il ne {___} pas venir à la fête.
(He will not be able to come to the party.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: He will not be able to come to the party.
facts.explanation: pourra — il/elle form of pouvoir. Irregular stem: pourr- + -a.

facts.quizQuestion:
Un jour je {___} voyager dans le monde entier.
(One day I will want to travel the whole world.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: One day I will want to travel the whole world.
facts.explanation: voudrai — je form of vouloir. Irregular stem: voudr- + -ai. The -l- of vouloir disappears.

facts.quizQuestion:
Dans six mois je {___} parler français.
(In six months I will know how to speak French.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: In six months I will know how to speak French.
facts.explanation: saurai — je form of savoir. Irregular stem: saur- + -ai. The -v- and -oi- of savoir contract to saur-.

facts.quizQuestion:
Tu {___} travailler dur pour réussir.
(You will have to work hard to succeed.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: You will have to work hard to succeed.
facts.explanation: devras — tu form of devoir. Irregular stem: devr- + -as. The -oi- of devoir contracts.

facts.quizQuestion:
Nous {___} si c'est une bonne idée.
(We will see if it's a good idea.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: We will see if it's a good idea.
facts.explanation: verrons — nous form of voir. Irregular stem: verr- + -ons. Double-r marks this stem.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je t'{___} un message dès que j'arrive.
(I will send you a message as soon as I arrive.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I will send you a message as soon as I arrive.
facts.explanation: enverrai — je form of envoyer. Irregular stem: enverr- + -ai. The -oy- of envoyer changes to -err-.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il {___} les résultats de son examen lundi.
(He will receive his exam results on Monday.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: He will receive his exam results on Monday.
facts.explanation: recevra — il/elle form of recevoir. Irregular stem: recevr- + -a. The -oi- contracts to -v- in the stem.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je {___} prends.
(I'll take him/it.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I'll take him/it.
facts.grammarPointLabel: COD pronoun le — replaces masculine singular direct object
facts.explanation: le replaces a masculine singular direct object noun placed before the verb. Je prends le livre → Je le prends.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je {___} vois rarement.
(I see him/it rarely.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I see him/it rarely.
facts.grammarPointLabel: COD pronoun le — replaces masculine singular direct object
facts.explanation: le replaces a masculine singular direct object before the verb. Je vois mon frère rarement → Je le vois rarement.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je {___}aime beaucoup.
(I like him/her/it very much.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I like him/her/it very much.
facts.grammarPointLabel: COD pronoun l' — elided form before vowel
facts.explanation: l' is the elided form of le or la used before a verb beginning with a vowel or silent h. J'aime ma ville → Je l'aime.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je {___}ai oublié.
(I forgot it.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I forgot it.
facts.grammarPointLabel: COD pronoun l' — elided form, passé composé position
facts.explanation: l' elides before the auxiliary in passé composé. J'ai oublié le code → Je l'ai oublié. The COD pronoun precedes the auxiliary even in compound tenses.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je {___}ai vu.
(I did see him.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I did see him.
facts.grammarPointLabel: COD pronoun l' — passé composé position
facts.explanation: l' replaces a masculine or feminine singular noun before the auxiliary in passé composé. J'ai vu Paul → Je l'ai vu.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je {___} connais.
(I know you.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I know you.
facts.grammarPointLabel: COD pronoun te — 2nd person singular direct object
facts.explanation: te (t' before a vowel) is the 2nd person singular COD pronoun. connaître quelqu'un takes no preposition, so the person is a direct object. Je connais toi → Je te connais.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je {___} remercie.
(I thank you.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I thank you.
facts.grammarPointLabel: COD pronoun te — remercier takes a direct object
facts.explanation: remercier quelqu'un uses no preposition, so the person thanked is the direct object (COD). Je remercie toi → Je te remercie.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je {___} crois.
(I believe you.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I believe you.
facts.grammarPointLabel: COD pronoun te — croire takes a direct object
facts.explanation: croire quelqu'un takes no preposition, so the person believed is a direct object (COD). Je crois toi → Je te crois.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il {___} cherche partout.
(He's looking for her everywhere.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: He's looking for her everywhere.
facts.grammarPointLabel: COD pronoun la — replaces feminine singular direct object
facts.explanation: la replaces a feminine singular direct object. Il cherche sa clé → Il la cherche. Elides to l' before a vowel or silent h.

facts.quizQuestion:
Tu {___} connais bien, cette ville ?
(Do you know this city well?)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Do you know this city well?
facts.grammarPointLabel: COD pronoun la — replaces feminine noun (ville)
facts.explanation: ville is feminine, so the COD pronoun is la. Tu connais cette ville → Tu la connais. The noun can move to the end (left dislocation) for emphasis.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je {___} vois tous les jours.
(I see them every day.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I see them every day.
facts.grammarPointLabel: COD pronoun les — replaces plural direct object (m/f)
facts.explanation: les replaces any plural direct object regardless of gender. Je vois mes amis → Je les vois. Position: before the conjugated verb.

facts.quizQuestion:
Elle ne {___} a pas invités.
(She didn't invite them.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: She didn't invite them.
facts.grammarPointLabel: COD pronoun les — passé composé with negation
facts.explanation: les precedes the auxiliary in passé composé. With negation: ne + pronoun + auxiliary + pas. Elle n'a pas invité ses amis → Elle ne les a pas invités. Past participle agrees with the preceding COD.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il {___} regarde.
(He's looking at me.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: He's looking at me.
facts.grammarPointLabel: COD pronoun me — 1st person singular direct object
facts.explanation: me (m' before vowel) is the 1st person singular COD pronoun. Il regarde moi → Il me regarde. Position: directly before the verb.

facts.quizQuestion:
Elle {___} attend à la gare.
(She's waiting for us at the station.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: She's waiting for us at the station.
facts.grammarPointLabel: COD pronoun nous — 1st person plural direct object
facts.explanation: nous as a COD replaces a 1st person plural direct object. attendre quelqu'un takes no preposition. Elle attend nous → Elle nous attend.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je {___} entends bien.
(I can hear you well.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I can hear you well.
facts.grammarPointLabel: COD pronoun vous — 2nd person plural/formal direct object
facts.explanation: vous as a COD replaces a 2nd person plural or formal singular direct object. entendre quelqu'un takes no preposition. Je vous entends.

facts.quizQuestion:
Prends-{___} !
(Take it!)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Take it!
facts.grammarPointLabel: COD pronoun — affirmative imperative position (after verb)
facts.explanation: In affirmative commands the COD pronoun follows the verb with a hyphen: Prends-le. In negative commands it returns before the verb: Ne le prends pas.

facts.quizQuestion:
Mange-{___} !
(Eat them!)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Eat them!
facts.explanation: After an affirmative imperative, the COD pronoun is attached with a hyphen: Mange-les. For me/te in imperative: Dis-moi (stressed form moi, not me).

facts.quizQuestion:
Je ne {___} comprends pas.
(I don't understand it/him.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I don't understand it/him.
facts.grammarPointLabel: COD pronoun le — negative sentence position
facts.explanation: In a negative sentence the COD pronoun still comes directly before the verb, inside the ne…pas frame: Je ne le comprends pas. Never: Je le ne comprends pas.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je veux {___} voir demain.
(I want to see him/it tomorrow.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I want to see him/it tomorrow.
facts.explanation: When the verb governing the pronoun is an infinitive, the pronoun goes immediately before that infinitive. Je veux voir Paul → Je veux le voir. Not: Je le veux voir.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je {___} regarde. (une émission = a show)
(I'm watching it.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I'm watching it. (une émission = a show)
facts.grammarPointLabel: COD pronoun la — regarder takes a direct object
facts.explanation: regarder quelque chose/quelqu'un uses no preposition, so it takes a direct object (COD). For feminine noun une émission → la. Je regarde l'émission → Je la regarde.

facts.quizQuestion:
Tu {___} as achetés au marché ?
(Did you buy them at the market?)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Did you buy them at the market?
facts.grammarPointLabel: COD pronoun les — past participle agreement in passé composé
facts.explanation: When a COD precedes the verb in passé composé (avoir), the past participle agrees in gender and number. Tu as acheté les légumes → Tu les as achetés (masc. plural → -s).

facts.quizQuestion:
Il {___}a rencontrée hier.
(He met her yesterday.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: He met her yesterday.
facts.grammarPointLabel: COD pronoun l' — past participle agreement (feminine)
facts.explanation: la elides to l' before the auxiliary a. Past participle agrees: rencontré + -e = rencontrée (la is feminine). Il a rencontré Marie → Il l'a rencontrée.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je n'ose pas {___} parler.
(I dare not speak to her.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I dare not speak to her.
facts.grammarPointLabel: COI pronoun lui — replaces à + person (3rd sg m/f)
facts.explanation: lui replaces à + a singular person (masculine or feminine) as an indirect object. parler à quelqu'un → lui parler. Unlike COD pronouns, lui is gender-neutral in the singular.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je {___} le donnerai.
(I will give it to you.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I will give it to you.
facts.grammarPointLabel: COI pronoun te — 2nd person singular indirect object
facts.explanation: te is both a COD and a COI pronoun. Here donner quelque chose à toi → Je te le donnerai. When two object pronouns appear, me/te/nous/vous (COI) come before le/la/les (COD).

facts.quizQuestion:
Je {___} le prêterai.
(I'll lend it to you.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I'll lend it to you.
facts.grammarPointLabel: COI pronoun te — prêter à quelqu'un
facts.explanation: prêter quelque chose à quelqu'un — the recipient is indirect (à + person). For 2nd person singular: prêter à toi → te. Je te le prêterai.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je {___} le prêterai. (speaking to a group)
(I'll lend it to you.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I'll lend it to you (plural/formal).
facts.grammarPointLabel: COI pronoun vous — 2nd person plural/formal indirect object
facts.explanation: vous as a COI replaces à + vous (formal or plural). Je prêterai le livre à vous → Je vous le prêterai. COI order: vous before le.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je vais {___} téléphoner ce soir.
(I'm going to call him/her tonight.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I'm going to call him/her tonight.
facts.grammarPointLabel: COI pronoun lui — téléphoner à quelqu'un
facts.explanation: téléphoner à quelqu'un takes an indirect object. Replace à Paul / à Marie with lui regardless of gender. Je vais téléphoner à Paul → Je vais lui téléphoner.

facts.quizQuestion:
Elle {___} a donné un cadeau.
(She gave him/her a gift.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: She gave him/her a gift.
facts.grammarPointLabel: COI pronoun lui — donner à quelqu'un
facts.explanation: donner quelque chose à quelqu'un — the recipient takes à, making it the indirect object. Replace à Paul with lui. Elle a donné un cadeau à Paul → Elle lui a donné un cadeau.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je vais {___} expliquer le problème.
(I'm going to explain the problem to them.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I'm going to explain the problem to them.
facts.grammarPointLabel: COI pronoun leur — replaces à + plural persons
facts.explanation: leur replaces à + plural persons as an indirect object. expliquer à mes amis → leur expliquer. Note: leur (COI, no -s) vs. les (COD, plural any gender).

facts.quizQuestion:
Nous {___} avons envoyé une lettre.
(We sent them a letter.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: We sent them a letter.
facts.grammarPointLabel: COI pronoun leur — envoyer à quelqu'un(s)
facts.explanation: envoyer quelque chose à quelqu'un takes an indirect object for the recipient. For plural: envoyer à eux/elles → leur envoyer. Nous avons envoyé une lettre à nos amis → Nous leur avons envoyé une lettre.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il {___} a dit la vérité.
(He told me the truth.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: He told me the truth.
facts.grammarPointLabel: COI pronoun me — dire à quelqu'un
facts.explanation: dire quelque chose à quelqu'un — the person told takes à (indirect object). For 1st person singular: dire à moi → me dire. Il a dit la vérité à moi → Il me a dit → Il m'a dit (elides before vowel).

facts.quizQuestion:
Il {___} a répondu très vite.
(He answered us very quickly.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: He answered us very quickly.
facts.grammarPointLabel: COI pronoun nous — répondre à quelqu'un
facts.explanation: répondre à quelqu'un uses an indirect object (à + person). For 1st person plural: répondre à nous → nous répondre. Il a répondu à nous → Il nous a répondu.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je ne {___} parle plus.
(I don't speak to him/her anymore.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I don't speak to him/her anymore.
facts.grammarPointLabel: COI pronoun lui — negative sentence position
facts.explanation: In a negative sentence the COI pronoun goes before the verb, inside the ne…plus frame: Je ne lui parle plus. parler à quelqu'un → lui (COI).

facts.quizQuestion:
Je dois {___} écrire.
(I must write to him/her.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I must write to him/her.
facts.explanation: When the object verb is an infinitive, the COI pronoun precedes it directly. écrire à quelqu'un → lui écrire. Je dois écrire à Paul → Je dois lui écrire.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je {___} parle souvent. (un ami = a friend)
(I talk to him often.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I talk to him often. (un ami = a friend)
facts.grammarPointLabel: COI pronoun lui — parler à quelqu'un (vs. COD)
facts.explanation: parler à quelqu'un uses à, making the person an indirect object (COI). Replace à mon ami with lui regardless of gender. Je parle à mon ami → Je lui parle. Compare: regarder quelqu'un (no à) → le/la (COD).

facts.quizQuestion:
Sam aide quiconque {___} demande.
(Sam helps whoever asks him.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Sam helps whoever asks him.
facts.grammarPointLabel: COI pronoun lui — demander à quelqu'un
facts.explanation: demander à quelqu'un = ask someone (à + person → COI). Replace à Sam with the COI pronoun lui. quiconque lui demande = whoever asks him.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je vais à Paris ce week-end. → J'{___} vais ce week-end.
(I'm going to Paris this weekend. → I'm going there this weekend.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I'm going there this weekend.
facts.explanation: y remplace « à Paris » (complément de lieu introduit par à). Il se place avant le verbe conjugué : J'y vais.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je suis souvent allé à cet endroit. → J'{___} suis souvent allé.
(I have often been to that place. → I have often been there.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I have often been there.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Pronom y — remplace à + lieu (passé composé)
facts.explanation: y remplace « à cet endroit » et se place avant l'auxiliaire au passé composé : J'y suis allé.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je suis également allé là-bas. → J'{___} suis également allé.
(I also went there.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I also went there.
facts.explanation: y remplace un complément de lieu (là-bas / à cet endroit). Position avant l'auxiliaire : J'y suis allé.

facts.quizQuestion:
Tu peux aller là-bas. → Tu peux {___} aller.
(You may go there.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: You may go there.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Pronom y — infinitif avec verbe modal
facts.explanation: y remplace le lieu et se place avant l'infinitif : Tu peux y aller. Avec un modal (pouvoir, vouloir…), y précède l'infinitif.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il habite dans cette ville. → Il {___} habite.
(He lives in that town. → He lives there.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: He lives there.
facts.explanation: y remplace « dans cette ville » (complément de lieu). Position avant le verbe : Il y habite.

facts.quizQuestion:
Elle est restée à la maison toute la journée. → Elle {___} est restée toute la journée.
(She stayed home all day. → She stayed there all day.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: She stayed there all day.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Pronom y — remplace à + lieu (passé composé, rester)
facts.explanation: y remplace « à la maison » et se place avant l'auxiliaire être : Elle y est restée.

facts.quizQuestion:
Nous sommes arrivés au bureau à neuf heures. → Nous {___} sommes arrivés à neuf heures.
(We arrived at the office at nine. → We arrived there at nine.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: We arrived there at nine.
facts.explanation: y remplace « au bureau » (à + le). Position avant l'auxiliaire : Nous y sommes arrivés.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je pense à ce problème tout le temps. → J'{___} pense tout le temps.
(I think about this problem all the time. → I think about it all the time.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I think about it all the time.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Pronom y — remplace à + chose (penser à)
facts.explanation: y peut remplacer à + chose (non-personne) avec penser à : Je pense à ça → J'y pense.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je réponds à ce message maintenant. → J'{___} réponds maintenant.
(I'm replying to this message now. → I'm replying to it now.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I'm replying to it now.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Pronom y — remplace à + chose (répondre à)
facts.explanation: y remplace à + chose (un message, une question) avec des verbes comme répondre à. Position avant le verbe : J'y réponds.

facts.quizQuestion:
Va au marché ! → {___}-va !
(Go to the market! → Go there!)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Go there!
facts.explanation: À l'impératif affirmatif, y se place APRÈS le verbe avec un trait d'union : Vas-y ! (le s est ajouté pour la liaison). Exception à la règle habituelle.

facts.quizQuestion:
On peut arriver à cet endroit en bus. → On peut {___} arriver en bus.
(You can get to that place by bus. → You can get there by bus.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: You can get there by bus.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Pronom y — arriver à + lieu avec modal
facts.explanation: y remplace « à cet endroit » et se place avant l'infinitif arriver : On peut y arriver.

facts.quizQuestion:
Nous allons au restaurant ce soir. → Nous {___} allons ce soir.
(We're going to the restaurant tonight. → We're going there tonight.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: We're going there tonight.
facts.explanation: y remplace « au restaurant » (à + le). Position avant le verbe : Nous y allons.

facts.quizQuestion:
Ils travaillent dans cette usine depuis dix ans. → Ils {___} travaillent depuis dix ans.
(They've worked in that factory for ten years. → They've worked there for ten years.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: They've worked there for ten years.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Pronom y — remplace dans + lieu (présent + depuis)
facts.explanation: y remplace « dans cette usine » (lieu). Position avant le verbe : Ils y travaillent.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je ne veux plus de café. → Je n'{___} veux plus.
(I don't want any more coffee. → I don't want any more.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I don't want any more.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Pronom en — remplace de + nom (partitif négatif)
facts.explanation: en remplace « de café » (article partitif). Position avant le verbe : Je n'en veux plus.

facts.quizQuestion:
J'en ai assez de cette situation. → J'{___} ai assez.
(I've had enough of this situation. → I've had enough of it.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I've had enough of it.
facts.explanation: en remplace « de cette situation » avec l'expression avoir assez de. Position avant le verbe : J'en ai assez.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je n'aime aucun de ces films. → Je n'{___} aime aucun.
(I don't like any of those films. → I don't like any of them.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I don't like any of them.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Pronom en — remplace de + nom avec aucun
facts.explanation: en remplace « de ces films » (groupe nominal avec de). Position avant le verbe : Je n'en aime aucun.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je ne veux pas plus de gâteau. → Je n'{___} veux pas plus.
(I don't want any more cake. → I don't want any more of it.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I don't want any more of it.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Pronom en — partitif négatif avec pas plus
facts.explanation: en remplace « de gâteau » (partitif). Position avant le verbe dans la négation : Je n'en veux pas plus.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il vient de Lyon. → Il {___} vient.
(He comes from Lyon. → He comes from there.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: He comes from there.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Pronom en — remplace de + lieu (venir de)
facts.explanation: en remplace « de Lyon » (lieu d'origine avec venir de). Position avant le verbe : Il en vient.

facts.quizQuestion:
J'ai acheté du pain ce matin. → J'{___} ai acheté ce matin.
(I bought some bread this morning. → I bought some this morning.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I bought some this morning.
facts.explanation: en remplace « du pain » (article partitif du). Position avant l'auxiliaire au passé composé : J'en ai acheté.

facts.quizQuestion:
Elle ne veut pas de soupe. → Elle n'{___} veut pas.
(She doesn't want any soup. → She doesn't want any.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: She doesn't want any.
facts.explanation: en remplace « de soupe » (partitif négatif). Position avant le verbe : Elle n'en veut pas.

facts.quizQuestion:
Prenez des fruits ! → Prenez-{___} !
(Take some fruit! → Take some!)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Take some!
facts.explanation: À l'impératif affirmatif, en se place APRÈS le verbe avec un trait d'union : Prenez-en ! Exception à la règle habituelle (avant le verbe).

facts.quizQuestion:
J'ai trois livres sur ce sujet. → J'{___} ai trois.
(I have three books on this topic. → I have three of them.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I have three of them.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Pronom en — avec expression de quantité
facts.explanation: en remplace « de livres » (complément de quantité). Le chiffre (trois) reste en fin de phrase : J'en ai trois.

facts.quizQuestion:
Tu as des frères et sœurs ? → Tu {___} as ?
(Do you have brothers and sisters? → Do you have any?)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Do you have any?
facts.explanation: en remplace « des frères et sœurs » (article indéfini pluriel des). Position avant le verbe : Tu en as ?

facts.quizQuestion:
Je mange de la soupe tous les soirs. → J'{___} mange tous les soirs.
(I eat soup every evening. → I eat some every evening.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I eat some every evening.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Pronom en — remplace de la + nom (partitif féminin)
facts.explanation: en remplace « de la soupe » (article partitif féminin). Position avant le verbe : J'en mange.

facts.quizQuestion:
Nous avons besoin de temps. → Nous {___} avons besoin.
(We need time. → We need some.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: We need some.
facts.explanation: en remplace « de temps » avec l'expression avoir besoin de. Position avant le verbe : Nous en avons besoin.

facts.quizQuestion:
Ce matin, je {___} à sept heures.
(This morning, I got up at seven o'clock.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: This morning, I got up at seven o'clock.
facts.explanation: Pronominal verbs take être in passé composé. Je + me + suis (être) + levé(e). Past participle agrees with subject: levé (masc.) or levée (fem.).

facts.quizQuestion:
À quelle heure tu {___} hier matin ?
(What time did you get up yesterday morning?)
facts.sentenceTranslation: What time did you get up yesterday morning?
facts.explanation: Tu + te (elided to t' before vowel) + es (être) + levé(e). Reflexive pronoun elides before the vowel of être.

facts.quizQuestion:
Marie {___} très tôt ce matin.
(Marie got up very early this morning.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Marie got up very early this morning.
facts.explanation: Elle + se (elided s') + est (être) + levée. Feminine subject: past participle adds -e giving levée.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je {___} précipitamment ce matin.
(I hurried my clothes on this morning.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I hurried my clothes on this morning.
facts.explanation: Je + me + suis (être) + habillé(e). Regular -er reflexive. Participle habillé agrees with subject; feminine adds -e: habillée.

facts.quizQuestion:
Elle {___} en cinq minutes.
(She got dressed in five minutes.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: She got dressed in five minutes.
facts.explanation: Elle + s'est + habillée. Feminine subject: participle agreement adds -e giving habillée.

facts.quizQuestion:
Sophie {___} à minuit hier soir.
(Sophie went to bed at midnight last night.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Sophie went to bed at midnight last night.
facts.explanation: Elle + s'est + couchée. Feminine subject: couché + -e = couchée. Pronominal verbs always use être.

facts.quizQuestion:
Nous {___} très tard après la fête.
(We went to bed very late after the party.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: We went to bed very late after the party.
facts.explanation: Nous + nous + sommes (être) + couchés. Mixed or masculine group: couchés. All-feminine group: couchées.

facts.quizQuestion:
Tu {___} plusieurs fois pendant la nuit.
(You woke up several times during the night.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: You woke up several times during the night.
facts.explanation: Tu + te (t' before vowel) + es + réveillé(e). Accent retained in réveillé. Feminine: réveillée.

facts.quizQuestion:
Les enfants {___} de bonne heure.
(The children woke up early.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: The children woke up early.
facts.explanation: Ils + se + sont (être) + réveillés. Masculine plural: past participle adds -s giving réveillés.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je {___} les mains avant de manger.
(I washed my hands before eating.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I washed my hands before eating.
facts.explanation: Je + me + suis + lavé(e). With a following direct object (les mains), the participle does NOT agree with the subject — lavé stays uninflected.

facts.quizQuestion:
Elle {___} avant de sortir.
(She washed up before going out.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: She washed up before going out.
facts.explanation: Elle + s'est + lavée. No following direct object, so the participle agrees with the feminine subject: lavée.

facts.quizQuestion:
Nous {___} dans le parc dimanche matin.
(We went for a walk in the park Sunday morning.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: We went for a walk in the park Sunday morning.
facts.explanation: Nous + nous + sommes + promenés. Mixed or masculine group: promenés. All-feminine group: promenées.

facts.quizQuestion:
Ils {___} au bord de la mer.
(They went for a walk by the sea.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: They went for a walk by the sea.
facts.explanation: Ils + se + sont + promenés. Masculine plural: promené + -s = promenés.

facts.quizQuestion:
Nous {___} beaucoup à la soirée.
(We had a lot of fun at the party.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: We had a lot of fun at the party.
facts.explanation: Nous + nous + sommes + amusés. Mixed or masculine group: amusés. All-feminine group: amusées.

facts.quizQuestion:
Les enfants {___} dans le jardin toute la journée.
(The children had fun in the garden all day.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: The children had fun in the garden all day.
facts.explanation: Ils + se + sont + amusés. Masculine plural group: amusé + -s = amusés.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je {___} son nom au dernier moment.
(I remembered his name at the last moment.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I remembered his name at the last moment.
facts.explanation: Je + me + suis + rappelé(e). Se rappeler (to remember) is pronominal and uses être. With following direct object (son nom), participle stays uninflected regardless of gender.

facts.quizQuestion:
Elle {___} de cet événement.
(She remembered that event.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: She remembered that event.
facts.explanation: Elle + s'est + rappelée. After se rappeler de, no direct object follows the verb, so the participle agrees with the feminine subject: rappelée.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je {___} de ses paroles exactement.
(I remembered his exact words.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I remembered his exact words.
facts.explanation: Je + me + suis + souvenu(e). Irregular participle from the venir family: souvenu. Feminine: souvenue.

facts.quizQuestion:
Elle {___} de sa première journée d'école.
(She remembered her first day of school.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: She remembered her first day of school.
facts.explanation: Elle + s'est + souvenue. Irregular participle souvenu agrees with feminine subject: souvenue.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il {___} devant la boulangerie.
(He stopped in front of the bakery.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: He stopped in front of the bakery.
facts.explanation: Il + s'est + arrêté. Masculine singular subject: no agreement suffix. Circumflex on â in arrêter.

facts.quizQuestion:
Elles {___} un moment pour se reposer.
(They stopped for a moment to rest.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: They stopped for a moment to rest.
facts.explanation: Elles + se + sont + arrêtées. Feminine plural: arrêté + -e + -s = arrêtées.

facts.quizQuestion:
Nous {___} vite pour ne pas rater le bus.
(We got dressed quickly so as not to miss the bus.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: We got dressed quickly so as not to miss the bus.
facts.explanation: Nous + nous + sommes + habillés. Mixed or masculine group: habillés. All-feminine group: habillées.

facts.quizQuestion:
Ils {___} pour applaudir l'acteur.
(They stood up to applaud the actor.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: They stood up to applaud the actor.
facts.explanation: Ils + se + sont + levés. Masculine plural: levé + -s = levés.

facts.quizQuestion:
Mon frère {___} avant vingt heures.
(My brother went to bed before eight o'clock.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: My brother went to bed before eight o'clock.
facts.explanation: Il + s'est + couché. Masculine singular: no agreement suffix. Couché stays couché.

facts.quizQuestion:
Cette ville est {___} grande que la mienne.
(This city is bigger than mine.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: This city is bigger than mine.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Comparatif de supériorité: plus + adj + que
facts.explanation: plus…que = comparative of superiority. Structure: plus + adjective + que + second term.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il parle plus vite {___} son frère.
(He speaks faster than his brother.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: He speaks faster than his brother.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Comparatif de supériorité: plus + adj + que
facts.explanation: que links the two terms in a comparison: plus vite que = faster than.

facts.quizQuestion:
Ce livre est {___} intéressant que le film.
(This book is less interesting than the film.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: This book is less interesting than the film.
facts.explanation: moins…que = comparative of inferiority. Structure: moins + adjective + que + second term.

facts.quizQuestion:
Le train est {___} l'avion.
(The train is cheaper than the plane.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: The train is cheaper than the plane.
facts.explanation: moins cher que = cheaper than. Structure: moins + adjective + que + noun.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il t'aime {___} que moi.
(He loves you as much as I do.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: He loves you as much as I do.
facts.explanation: aussi…que = comparative of equality. Structure: aussi + adjective + que + second term.

facts.quizQuestion:
Viens {___} possible.
(Come as soon as possible.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Come as soon as possible.
facts.explanation: aussi vite que = as fast as. Works with adjectives and adverbs: aussi vite que possible.

facts.quizQuestion:
Ma sœur est {___} mon frère.
(My sister is as intelligent as my brother.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: My sister is as intelligent as my brother.
facts.explanation: aussi intelligente que = as intelligent as. The adjective agrees with the subject: sœur (f.) → intelligente.

facts.quizQuestion:
Paris est {___} Lyon.
(Paris is bigger than Lyon.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Paris is bigger than Lyon.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Comparatif de supériorité: plus + adj + que
facts.explanation: plus grand que = bigger than. No separate comparative adjective in French — use plus + base adjective.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il a {___} patience que moi.
(He has more patience than me.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: He has more patience than me.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Comparatif avec noms: plus de + nom + que
facts.explanation: For comparing quantities of nouns, use plus de…que (not plus…que). Plus de replaces 'more' before a noun.

facts.quizQuestion:
Elle gagne {___} argent que son patron.
(She earns less money than her boss.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: She earns less money than her boss.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Comparatif avec noms: moins de + nom + que
facts.explanation: moins de…que = less/fewer… than (with nouns). Pattern: moins de + noun + que + second term.

facts.quizQuestion:
Tu as {___} livres que moi.
(You have as many books as I do.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: You have as many books as I do.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Comparatif avec noms: autant de + nom + que
facts.explanation: autant de…que = as much/many… as (with nouns). Note: with nouns use autant de, not aussi.

facts.quizQuestion:
C'est {___} robe du magasin.
(It's the most beautiful dress in the shop.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: It's the most beautiful dress in the shop.
facts.explanation: la plus belle = the most beautiful (f. sg.). Superlative: article (agreeing) + plus/moins + adjective.

facts.quizQuestion:
C'est {___} bâtiment de la ville.
(It's the tallest building in the city.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: It's the tallest building in the city.
facts.explanation: le plus grand = the tallest/biggest (m. sg.). The article agrees in gender and number with the noun.

facts.quizQuestion:
Cherchez {___} hôtel de la région.
(Look for the cheapest hotel in the area.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Look for the cheapest hotel in the area.
facts.explanation: le moins cher = the cheapest (superlative of inferiority). Use article + moins + adjective.

facts.quizQuestion:
Ce sont {___} coureurs du monde.
(They are the fastest runners in the world.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: They are the fastest runners in the world.
facts.explanation: les plus rapides = the fastest (pl.). Plural superlative: les (pl.) + plus + adjective in plural form.

facts.quizQuestion:
C'est le restaurant le plus cher {___} la ville.
(It's the most expensive restaurant in the city.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: It's the most expensive restaurant in the city.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Superlatif + groupe: le plus + adj + de + groupe
facts.explanation: After the superlative, the group/scope is introduced by de: le plus cher de la ville = the most expensive in the city.

facts.quizQuestion:
Ce café est {___} que celui d'hier.
(This coffee is better than yesterday's.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: This coffee is better than yesterday's.
facts.explanation: meilleur = better (comparative of bon/good). Never say plus bon — only meilleur is correct.

facts.quizQuestion:
Sa pizza est {___} que la mienne.
(Her pizza is better than mine.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Her pizza is better than mine.
facts.explanation: meilleure = better (f. sg.). meilleur agrees in gender: bon (m.) → meilleur / bonne (f.) → meilleure.

facts.quizQuestion:
Jean est mon {___} ami.
(Jean is my best friend.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Jean is my best friend.
facts.explanation: meilleur ami = best friend. When used with a possessive (mon, ma), no article needed: mon meilleur ami.

facts.quizQuestion:
Espérons {___}.
(Let's hope for the best.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Let's hope for the best.
facts.explanation: le meilleur = the best (m. sg. superlative of bon). Superlative form uses definite article: le/la/les meilleur(e)(s).

facts.quizQuestion:
Cette situation est {___} qu'avant.
(This situation is worse than before.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: This situation is worse than before.
facts.explanation: pire = worse (comparative of mauvais/bad). pire is preferred; plus mauvais is also acceptable but less common.

facts.quizQuestion:
C'est {___} film que j'aie jamais vu.
(It's the worst film I've ever seen.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: It's the worst film I've ever seen.
facts.explanation: le pire = the worst (m. sg. superlative of mauvais). Superlative: le/la/les pire(s).

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} vaut tard que jamais.
(Better late than never.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Better late than never.
facts.explanation: mieux = better (comparative of bien/well, adverb). Never say plus bien — only mieux is correct.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je chante {___} que toi.
(I sing better than you.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I sing better than you.
facts.explanation: mieux = better (adverb). It compares how well someone does something. mieux que = better than.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il parle {___} que sa sœur.
(He speaks less well than his sister.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: He speaks less well than his sister.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Comparatif d'infériorité de bien: moins bien
facts.explanation: moins bien = less well. For the adverb bien, inferiority uses moins bien (not pire, which is for mauvais).

facts.quizQuestion:
Ce vin est {___} que celui-là. (adj — comparer le vin)
(This wine is better than that one.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: This wine is better than that one.
facts.explanation: meilleur = better as an adjective (replaces bon). It modifies a noun and agrees: meilleur / meilleure / meilleurs / meilleures.

facts.quizQuestion:
Elle joue au tennis {___} que moi. (adv — comparer comment elle joue)
(She plays tennis better than me.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: She plays tennis better than me.
facts.explanation: mieux = better as an adverb (replaces bien). It modifies a verb and never changes form: joue mieux = plays better.

facts.quizQuestion:
C'est {___} question de l'examen.
(It's the least difficult question on the exam.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: It's the least difficult question on the exam.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Superlatif d'infériorité: la + moins + adj (f. sg.)
facts.explanation: la moins difficile = the least difficult (f. sg.). Article must agree with the noun: la question → la moins difficile.

facts.quizQuestion:
La voiture est {___} le vélo.
(The car is faster than the bicycle.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: The car is faster than the bicycle.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Comparatif de supériorité: plus + adj + que
facts.explanation: plus rapide que = faster than. French uses plus + base adjective (rapide) + que — no separate comparative form.

facts.quizQuestion:
