Ce sont {___} chaussures du magasin.
(They are the least expensive shoes in the shop.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: They are the least expensive shoes in the shop.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Superlatif d'infériorité pluriel: les + moins + adj (f. pl.)
facts.explanation: les moins chères = the least expensive (f. pl.). Adjective agrees in gender and number: chaussures (f. pl.) → chères.

facts.quizQuestion:
Ce restaurant est {___} l'autre.
(This restaurant is as good as the other one.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: This restaurant is as good as the other one.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Comparatif d'égalité avec bon: aussi bon que
facts.explanation: aussi bon que = as good as. Note: equality with bon uses aussi bon que, not aussi meilleur que.

facts.quizQuestion:
Ces fleurs sont {___} que celles d'avant.
(These flowers are better than the previous ones.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: These flowers are better than the previous ones.
facts.explanation: meilleures = better (f. pl.). meilleur agrees fully: meilleur (m. sg.) / meilleure (f. sg.) / meilleurs (m. pl.) / meilleures (f. pl.).

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} plus doucement, s'il te plaît.
(Speak more quietly, please.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Speak more quietly, please.
facts.explanation: parle — tu imperative of parler. Regular -er verb: drop the infinitive -er, add nothing (and drop the final -s of the present-tense tu form: parles → parle).

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} tes légumes !
(Eat your vegetables!)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Eat your vegetables!
facts.explanation: mange — tu imperative of manger. Regular -er verb: present tu form manges → drop the -s → mange.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} au lit tout de suite !
(Go to bed right now!)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Go to bed right now!
facts.explanation: va — tu imperative of aller. Irregular: aller is an -er verb but the tu form is va (not vas — the -s is dropped in the imperative, except before y: vas-y).

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} tes devoirs avant de sortir.
(Finish your homework before going out.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Finish your homework before going out.
facts.explanation: finis — tu imperative of finir. Regular -ir verb: same as the present indicative tu form (the final -s is kept for -ir verbs).

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} maintenant ou tu seras en retard.
(Leave now or you'll be late.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Leave now or you'll be late.
facts.explanation: pars — tu imperative of partir. Irregular -ir verb (not -iss- type): same as present indicative tu form, keeping the final -s.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} l'autre chaise !
(Take the other chair!)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Take the other chair!
facts.explanation: prends — tu imperative of prendre. Regular -re verb: same as the present indicative tu form, keeping the final -s.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} une minute, je reviens.
(Wait a minute, I'll be right back.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Wait a minute, I'll be right back.
facts.explanation: attends — tu imperative of attendre. Regular -re verb: same as present indicative tu form, keeping the final -s.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} ton lait.
(Drink up your milk.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Drink up your milk.
facts.explanation: bois — tu imperative of boire. Irregular verb: same as the present indicative tu form (tu bois), keeping the final -s.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} ici, John.
(Come here, John.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Come here, John.
facts.explanation: viens — tu imperative of venir. Irregular -ir verb (not -iss- type): same as present indicative tu form, keeping the final -s.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} courageux !
(Be brave!)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Be brave!
facts.explanation: sois — tu imperative of être. Fully irregular: from the present subjunctive (que tu sois). Not derived from the present indicative.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} confiance en toi !
(Have confidence in yourself!)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Have confidence in yourself!
facts.explanation: aie — tu imperative of avoir. Fully irregular: from the present subjunctive (que tu aies) with the final -s dropped.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} que nous sommes là pour toi.
(Know that we are here for you.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Know that we are here for you.
facts.explanation: sache — tu imperative of savoir. Fully irregular: from the present subjunctive (que tu saches) with the final -s dropped.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} plus fort — personne ne nous entend.
(Let's speak louder — no one can hear us.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Let's speak louder — no one can hear us.
facts.explanation: parlons — nous imperative of parler. Identical to the present indicative nous form (nous parlons). Used to suggest an action together.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} plus lentement, s'il vous plaît.
(Speak more slowly, please.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Speak more slowly, please.
facts.explanation: parlez — vous imperative of parler. Identical to the present indicative vous form (vous parlez). Used for formal commands or addressing multiple people.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} ce travail avant midi.
(Let's finish this work before noon.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Let's finish this work before noon.
facts.explanation: finissons — nous imperative of finir. Identical to the present indicative nous form (nous finissons). Regular -ir verb with -iss- infix.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} vos exercices pour demain.
(Finish your exercises for tomorrow.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Finish your exercises for tomorrow.
facts.explanation: finissez — vous imperative of finir. Identical to the present indicative vous form (vous finissez).

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} le bus — c'est plus rapide.
(Let's take the bus — it's faster.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Let's take the bus — it's faster.
facts.explanation: prenons — nous imperative of prendre. Identical to the present indicative nous form (nous prenons).

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} votre temps, il n'y a pas d'urgence.
(Take your time, there's no rush.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Take your time, there's no rush.
facts.explanation: prenez — vous imperative of prendre. Identical to the present indicative vous form (vous prenez).

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} patients — ça prend du temps.
(Let's be patient — it takes time.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Let's be patient — it takes time.
facts.explanation: soyons — nous imperative of être. Fully irregular: from the present subjunctive (que nous soyons). Negative: ne soyons pas.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} à l'heure demain matin.
(Be on time tomorrow morning.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Be on time tomorrow morning.
facts.explanation: soyez — vous imperative of être. Fully irregular: from the present subjunctive (que vous soyez). Negative: ne soyez pas.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} du courage !
(Let's have courage!)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Let's have courage!
facts.explanation: ayons — nous imperative of avoir. Fully irregular: from the present subjunctive (que nous ayons). Negative: n'ayons pas.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} la bonté de fermer la porte.
(Be so kind as to close the door.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Be so kind as to close the door.
facts.explanation: ayez — vous imperative of avoir. Fully irregular: from the present subjunctive (que vous ayez). Negative: n'ayez pas.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} reconnaître nos erreurs.
(Let's know how to recognize our mistakes.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Let's know how to recognize our mistakes.
facts.explanation: sachons — nous imperative of savoir. Fully irregular: from the present subjunctive (que nous sachions), truncated to sachons.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} que la réunion est annulée.
(Know that the meeting is cancelled.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Know that the meeting is cancelled.
facts.explanation: sachez — vous imperative of savoir. Fully irregular: from the present subjunctive (que vous sachiez), truncated to sachez.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} si fort — le bébé dort.
(Don't speak so loudly — the baby is sleeping.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Don't speak so loudly — the baby is sleeping.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Impératif négatif tu: ne + parle + pas
facts.explanation: ne parle pas — negative tu imperative of parler. Structure: ne + imperative form + pas. Note: the affirmative tu form parle (no -s) is used unchanged inside the negative.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} encore — le film n'est pas fini.
(Don't leave yet — the film isn't over.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Don't leave yet — the film isn't over.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Impératif négatif vous: ne + partez + pas
facts.explanation: ne partez pas — negative vous imperative of partir. Structure: ne + imperative form (partez) + pas. The imperative form is unchanged.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} — il est déjà huit heures !
(Get up — it's already eight o'clock!)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Get up — it's already eight o'clock!
facts.grammarPointLabel: Impératif pronominal tu: se lever → lève-toi
facts.explanation: lève-toi — tu affirmative imperative of se lever. In affirmative pronominal imperatives, the reflexive pronoun follows the verb with a hyphen, and te becomes toi.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} — la cérémonie va commencer.
(Let's stand up — the ceremony is about to begin.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Let's stand up — the ceremony is about to begin.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Impératif pronominal nous: se lever → levons-nous
facts.explanation: levons-nous — nous affirmative imperative of se lever. The reflexive pronoun nous follows the verb with a hyphen.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___}, s'il vous plaît.
(Stand up, please.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Stand up, please.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Impératif pronominal vous: se lever → levez-vous
facts.explanation: levez-vous — vous affirmative imperative of se lever. The reflexive pronoun vous follows the verb with a hyphen. This is the Tatoeba source sentence.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} — reste au lit, tu es malade.
(Don't get up — stay in bed, you're sick.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Don't get up — stay in bed, you're sick.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Impératif pronominal négatif tu: ne te + lève + pas
facts.explanation: ne te lève pas — negative tu imperative of se lever. In negative pronominal imperatives, the reflexive pronoun reverts to te (not toi) and stays before the verb: ne + te + imperative + pas.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} avant la fin du spectacle.
(Let's not stand up before the end of the show.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Let's not stand up before the end of the show.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Impératif pronominal négatif nous: ne nous + levons + pas
facts.explanation: ne nous levons pas — negative nous imperative of se lever. Structure: ne + nous + imperative form + pas. The reflexive pronoun stays before the verb in the negative.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je {___} aller nager cet après-midi.
(I would like to go swimming this afternoon.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I would like to go swimming this afternoon.
facts.explanation: voudrais — je form of vouloir in the conditionnel. Stem: voudr-. Used for polite requests and wishes instead of the more abrupt je veux.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je {___} la rencontrer un jour.
(I would like to meet her one day.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I would like to meet her one day.
facts.explanation: voudrais — je form of vouloir (conditionnel). Softens the request compared to je veux, expressing a polite wish.

facts.quizQuestion:
Tu {___} venir avec nous ce soir ?
(Would you like to come with us tonight?)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Would you like to come with us tonight?
facts.explanation: voudrais — tu form of vouloir (conditionnel). Identical in spelling to je voudrais; context determines person. Used to politely invite.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il {___} un café, s'il vous plaît.
(He would like a coffee, please.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: He would like a coffee, please.
facts.explanation: voudrait — il/elle form of vouloir (conditionnel). Stem voudr- + -ait. Common in restaurant or polite service contexts.

facts.quizQuestion:
Nous {___} réserver une table pour deux personnes.
(We would like to book a table for two.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: We would like to book a table for two.
facts.explanation: voudrions — nous form of vouloir (conditionnel). Stem voudr- + -ions. Used for collective polite requests.

facts.quizQuestion:
Vous {___} quelque chose à boire ?
(Would you like something to drink?)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Would you like something to drink?
facts.explanation: voudriez — vous form of vouloir (conditionnel). Stem voudr- + -iez. Very common in service and hospitality French.

facts.quizQuestion:
Ils {___} visiter le musée demain.
(They would like to visit the museum tomorrow.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: They would like to visit the museum tomorrow.
facts.explanation: voudraient — ils/elles form of vouloir (conditionnel). Stem voudr- + -aient. Expresses a group's polite wish.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je {___} vous aider si vous voulez.
(I could help you if you like.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I could help you if you like.
facts.explanation: pourrais — je form of pouvoir (conditionnel). Stem pourr- + -ais. Used to offer help or make polite requests: 'Est-ce que je pourrais…?'

facts.quizQuestion:
Tu {___} fermer la porte, s'il te plaît ?
(Could you close the door, please?)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Could you close the door, please?
facts.explanation: pourrais — tu form of pouvoir (conditionnel). Identical spelling to je pourrais. Polite alternative to 'Ferme la porte !'

facts.quizQuestion:
Il {___} partir plus tôt si nécessaire.
(He could leave earlier if necessary.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: He could leave earlier if necessary.
facts.explanation: pourrait — il/elle form of pouvoir (conditionnel). Stem pourr- + -ait. Expresses possibility or polite permission.

facts.quizQuestion:
Nous {___} se retrouver au café demain matin.
(We could meet at the café tomorrow morning.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: We could meet at the café tomorrow morning.
facts.explanation: pourrions — nous form of pouvoir (conditionnel). Stem pourr- + -ions. Used to suggest possibilities politely.

facts.quizQuestion:
Vous {___} répéter la question, s'il vous plaît ?
(Could you repeat the question, please?)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Could you repeat the question, please?
facts.explanation: pourriez — vous form of pouvoir (conditionnel). Stem pourr- + -iez. Very common polite request formula in formal French.

facts.quizQuestion:
Ils {___} organiser la réunion la semaine prochaine.
(They could organise the meeting next week.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: They could organise the meeting next week.
facts.explanation: pourraient — ils/elles form of pouvoir (conditionnel). Stem pourr- + -aient. Expresses a polite collective possibility.

facts.quizQuestion:
J'{___} être guitariste un jour.
(I would like to be a guitarist one day.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I would like to be a guitarist one day.
facts.explanation: aimerais — je form of aimer (conditionnel). Regular verb: stem aimer- + -ais. Expresses a personal wish or aspiration.

facts.quizQuestion:
J'{___} aller à Londres pour les vacances.
(I would like to go to London for the holidays.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I would like to go to London for the holidays.
facts.explanation: aimerais — je form of aimer (conditionnel). Softer and more wishful than je veux; common for travel plans and personal desires.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il {___} partir tôt pour éviter les embouteillages.
(He would like to leave early to avoid traffic.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: He would like to leave early to avoid traffic.
facts.explanation: aimerait — il/elle form of aimer (conditionnel). Stem aimer- + -ait. Expresses a third-person wish or preference politely.

facts.quizQuestion:
Nous {___} rester un peu plus longtemps.
(We would like to stay a little longer.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: We would like to stay a little longer.
facts.explanation: aimerions — nous form of aimer (conditionnel). Stem aimer- + -ions. Used to express a collective wish politely.

facts.quizQuestion:
Vous {___} regarder la télévision ce soir ?
(Would you like to watch TV this evening?)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Would you like to watch TV this evening?
facts.explanation: aimeriez — vous form of aimer (conditionnel). Stem aimer- + -iez. A polite way to inquire about someone's preferences.

facts.quizQuestion:
Ils {___} visiter la tour Eiffel pendant leur séjour.
(They would like to visit the Eiffel Tower during their stay.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: They would like to visit the Eiffel Tower during their stay.
facts.explanation: aimeraient — ils/elles form of aimer (conditionnel). Stem aimer- + -aient. Expresses a third-person plural wish.

facts.quizQuestion:
Complete the real condition sentence: Si tu {___}, je viens.
(If you want, I'll come.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: If you want, I'll come.
facts.explanation: Si + présent expresses a real, achievable condition. 'Si tu veux' (if you want) is followed by a présent or futur: je viens or je viendrai. NOT conditionnel after si.

facts.correctAnswer: Si j'ai le temps, je téléphonerai.
acceptableAlternatives: Si j'ai le temps, je téléphonerai
facts.quizQuestion:
Which sentence correctly uses si + présent for a real condition?
(A) Si j'aurai le temps, je téléphone.
(B) Si j'ai le temps, je téléphonerai.
(C) Si j'aurais le temps, je téléphonerai.
(D) Si j'avais le temps, je téléphonerai.
facts.sentenceTranslation: If I have time, I will call.
facts.explanation: Si j'ai le temps, je téléphonerai — si is followed by présent (ai), and the result clause uses futur simple (téléphonerai). Never use futur or conditionnel directly after si.

facts.correctAnswer: Si il fait beau, je sortirai.
facts.quizQuestion:
Complétez avec la bonne structure : Si il {___} beau, je sortirai.
(If the weather is nice, I will go out.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: If the weather is nice, I will go out.
facts.explanation: Si il fait beau — si + présent (fait) introduces a real condition. Result clause uses futur simple (sortirai). Note: spoken French often contracts to S'il.

facts.correctAnswer: Si vous avez des questions, appelez-moi.
acceptableAlternatives: Si vous avez des questions, appelez-moi
facts.quizQuestion:
Which correctly combines si + présent with an imperatif result?
(A) Si vous aurez des questions, appelez-moi.
(B) Si vous avez des questions, appelez-moi.
(C) Si vous aviez des questions, appelez-moi.
(D) Si vous avez des questions, vous appeliez.
facts.sentenceTranslation: If you have any questions, call me.
facts.explanation: Si vous avez des questions — si + présent can be followed by an impératif in the result clause. Here: si + avez (présent) → appelez-moi (impératif).

facts.quizQuestion:
Complete the real condition: Si je {___}, j'irai.
(If I can, I will go.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: If I can, I will go.
facts.explanation: Si je peux — pouvoir (présent) after si. Result clause: futur simple (j'irai). This short pattern is extremely common in everyday French.

facts.correctAnswer: Si elle arrive tôt, nous partirons.
acceptableAlternatives: Si elle arrive tôt, nous partirons
facts.quizQuestion:
Complétez : Si elle {___} tôt, nous partirons.
(If she arrives early, we will leave.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: If she arrives early, we will leave.
facts.explanation: Si elle arrive — arrive is présent (not futur or conditionnel). Result clause uses futur simple: partirons. A hallmark A2 real-condition pattern.

facts.quizQuestion:
Quelle phrase utilise correctement si + présent + impératif ?
(A) Si tu seras fatigué, repose-toi.
(B) Si tu étais fatigué, repose-toi.
(C) Si tu es fatigué, repose-toi.
(D) Si tu sois fatigué, repose-toi.
facts.sentenceTranslation: If you are tired, rest.
facts.explanation: Si tu es fatigué — être in présent (es) after si. Result: impératif (repose-toi). Never use futur/conditionnel/subjonctif directly after si in real conditions.

facts.quizQuestion:
J'aime le café, {___} je préfère le thé le soir.
(I like coffee, but I prefer tea in the evening.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I like coffee, but I prefer tea in the evening.
facts.explanation: mais (but) introduces a contrast or opposition between two ideas.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il pleuvait, {___} nous sommes restés à la maison.
(It was raining, so we stayed home.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: It was raining, so we stayed home.
facts.explanation: donc (so / therefore) introduces a logical consequence from the preceding clause.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je suis fatigué {___} j'ai travaillé toute la nuit.
(I'm tired because I worked all night.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I'm tired because I worked all night.
facts.explanation: parce que (because) introduces the cause or reason for the main clause and is followed by a full subject-verb clause.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il lisait {___} elle cuisinait.
(He was reading while she was cooking.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: He was reading while she was cooking.
facts.explanation: pendant que (while) connects two simultaneous actions happening over the same period.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} j'étais enfant, j'habitais à Lyon.
(When I was a child, I lived in Lyon.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: When I was a child, I lived in Lyon.
facts.explanation: quand (when) introduces a time clause situating the action of the main clause.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je prends un manteau {___} il fait froid.
(I'm taking a coat because it is cold.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I'm taking a coat because it is cold.
facts.explanation: car (for / because) gives a reason, similar to parce que but more formal and never used to start a sentence.

facts.quizQuestion:
J'ai pris mon petit-déjeuner, {___} je suis sorti.
(I had breakfast, then I went out.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I had breakfast, then I went out.
facts.explanation: puis (then / next) sequences events in chronological order, typically in narration.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il n'avait pas de clé, {___} il a appelé un serrurier.
(He had no key, so he called a locksmith.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: He had no key, so he called a locksmith.
facts.explanation: alors (so / then) marks a consequence or the next step in a narrative sequence.

facts.quizQuestion:
D'abord on visite le musée, {___} on déjeune au restaurant.
(First we visit the museum, then we have lunch at the restaurant.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: First we visit the museum, then we have lunch at the restaurant.
facts.explanation: ensuite (then / afterwards) indicates the next step in a sequence, often paired with d'abord.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___}, il faut prendre le métro, puis changer à Châtelet.
(First, you take the metro, then change at Châtelet.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: First, you take the metro, then change at Châtelet.
facts.explanation: d'abord (first / first of all) opens an ordered sequence and typically precedes ensuite or puis.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il a répondu très {___} à ma question.
(He answered my question very quickly.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: He answered my question very quickly.
facts.explanation: rapidement (quickly) is formed from rapide → feminine rapide + -ment (adjective ends in -e, no change needed).

facts.quizQuestion:
Le professeur parle {___} pour que tout le monde comprenne.
(The teacher speaks slowly so that everyone understands.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: The teacher speaks slowly so that everyone understands.
facts.explanation: lentement (slowly) is formed from lent → feminine lente + -ment.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il a frappé {___} à la porte pour ne pas réveiller les enfants.
(He knocked gently on the door so as not to wake the children.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: He knocked gently on the door so as not to wake the children.
facts.explanation: doucement (gently / softly) is formed from doux → feminine douce + -ment.

facts.quizQuestion:
J'aime {___} nager dans la mer.
(I really like swimming in the sea.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I really like swimming in the sea.
facts.explanation: vraiment (really / truly) is formed from vrai → feminine vraie + -ment.

facts.quizQuestion:
Elle apprend les langues {___}.
(She learns languages easily.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: She learns languages easily.
facts.explanation: facilement (easily) is formed from facile → feminine facile + -ment (adjective ends in -e, suffix appended directly).

facts.quizQuestion:
Ce texte se lit {___} sans dictionnaire.
(This text is read with difficulty without a dictionary.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: This text is read with difficulty without a dictionary.
facts.explanation: difficilement (with difficulty) is formed from difficile → feminine difficile + -ment.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___}, personne n'a été blessé dans l'accident.
(Fortunately, nobody was hurt in the accident.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Fortunately, nobody was hurt in the accident.
facts.explanation: heureusement (fortunately) is formed from heureux → feminine heureuse + -ment.

facts.quizQuestion:
C'est {___} vrai.
(It is unfortunately true.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: It is unfortunately true.
facts.explanation: malheureusement (unfortunately) is formed from malheureux → feminine malheureuse + -ment.

facts.quizQuestion:
J'ai pris froid {___}.
(I caught a cold yesterday.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I caught a cold yesterday.
facts.explanation: hier (yesterday) is a time marker that triggers the passé composé for a completed action.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il est arrivé {___}, pas hier.
(He arrived the day before yesterday, not yesterday.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: He arrived the day before yesterday, not yesterday.
facts.explanation: avant-hier (the day before yesterday) refers to two days ago and is used with passé composé.

facts.quizQuestion:
Nous avons visité le Louvre {___}.
(We visited the Louvre last week.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: We visited the Louvre last week.
facts.explanation: la semaine dernière (last week) specifies a completed past period and collocates with the passé composé.

facts.quizQuestion:
Ils ont déménagé à Paris {___}.
(They moved to Paris last year.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: They moved to Paris last year.
facts.explanation: l'année dernière (last year) denotes a completed past year and is used with the passé composé.

facts.quizQuestion:
J'ai appris à conduire {___}.
(I learned to drive two years ago.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I learned to drive two years ago.
facts.grammarPointLabel: il y a + duration — Time expression (ago)
facts.explanation: il y a + duration (two years ago) indicates elapsed time since a past event and is used with passé composé.

facts.quizQuestion:
J'habite ici {___} trois ans.
(I have been living here for three years.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I have been living here for three years.
facts.grammarPointLabel: depuis — Ongoing duration from the past
facts.explanation: depuis (since / for) with the present tense expresses a situation that started in the past and is still ongoing.

facts.quizQuestion:
J'attends {___} des heures !
(I have been waiting for hours!)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I have been waiting for hours!
facts.explanation: depuis (for) with present tense shows an action still in progress; the waiting started in the past and continues now.

facts.quizQuestion:
J'ai dormi {___} neuf heures.
(I slept for nine hours.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I slept for nine hours.
facts.explanation: pendant (for) with passé composé expresses a completed duration; the sleeping is finished.

facts.quizQuestion:
J'ai lu {___} une heure avant de dormir.
(I read for an hour before sleeping.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I read for an hour before sleeping.
facts.explanation: pendant (for) marks a closed, finished span of time used with passé composé or imparfait.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je l'ai rencontré {___} une semaine.
(I met him a week ago.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I met him a week ago.
facts.explanation: il y a + duration (ago) measures time elapsed since a completed past event; used with passé composé.

facts.quizQuestion:
Le train part {___} dix minutes.
(The train leaves in ten minutes.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: The train leaves in ten minutes.
facts.explanation: dans + duration (in X time) points forward from the present moment to a future event, the opposite of il y a.

facts.quizQuestion:
C'est l'homme {___} habite en face de chez moi.
(That's the man who lives across from me.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: That's the man who lives across from me.
facts.explanation: qui (who / which) is the relative pronoun used when the antecedent is the subject of the relative clause.

facts.quizQuestion:
Ceux {___} le connaissent l'apprécient.
(Those who know him like him.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Those who know him like him.
facts.explanation: qui acts as the subject of the relative clause and can refer to people or things.

facts.quizQuestion:
C'est le film {___} j'ai vu hier soir.
(That's the film that I watched last night.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: That's the film that I watched last night.
facts.explanation: que (that / which / whom) is used when the antecedent is the direct object of the relative clause.

facts.quizQuestion:
C'est l'ami {___} elle a rencontré à Paris.
(That's the friend that she met in Paris.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: That's the friend that she met in Paris.
facts.grammarPointLabel: qu' — Elision of que before vowel
facts.explanation: que elides to qu' before a word beginning with a vowel or silent h; the function (direct object) remains the same.

facts.quizQuestion:
Le livre {___} je lis est très intéressant.
(The book that I am reading is very interesting.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: The book that I am reading is very interesting.
facts.explanation: que links the relative clause where le livre is the direct object of lire; the subject je follows immediately after que.

facts.quizQuestion:
J'ai un chat {___} adore dormir sur le canapé.
(I have a cat that loves sleeping on the sofa.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I have a cat that loves sleeping on the sofa.
facts.explanation: qui can refer to animals and inanimate things as well as people when it functions as the subject of the relative clause.

chainThemes.name: Pronoms Y & En
chainThemes.description: Pronom y — replacing location or à + thing; pronom en — replacing de + noun or partitive

chainThemes.name: Verbes Pronominaux au Passé
chainThemes.description: Pronominal verbs in passé composé with être: se lever, s'habiller, se coucher + participle agreement

chainThemes.name: Comparatifs & Superlatifs
chainThemes.description: Comparatifs (plus/moins/aussi…que), superlatifs (le/la plus), and irregular forms (meilleur, pire, mieux)

chainThemes.name: Impératif & Structures
chainThemes.description: Impératif (tu/nous/vous), conditionnel de politesse (voudrais, aimerais), si + présent, connecteurs logiques
